title: Pentagon unveils trove of declassfied 'UFO' videos. How to see them all, from 'a football-shaped body' to 'a misshapen and uneven ball of white light'
summary: The Pentagon released nearly 30 declassified UFO videos, including footage of objects described as a 'football-shaped body' and 'a misshapen and uneven ball of white light'. The documents were unveiled on May 8 as part of a new tranche of UFO files.

title: Black holes slamming into scorching stars may be causing mysterious blue flashes in the cosmos
summary: Luminous Fast Blue Optical Transients (LFBOTs) may be caused by black holes or neutron stars colliding with the universe's hottest stars. These rare blue cosmic explosions have puzzled astronomers for years.

title: Monopoly sucks, but this new 'Star Wars' video game spinoff might add some galactic sizzle
summary: A new 'Star Wars' video game spinoff is announced, potentially adding excitement to the gaming industry. The title references a classic Monopoly line, hinting at its nostalgic appeal.

title: How you'd really die in space: What sci-fi gets right (and wrong) about extra-terrestrial expirations
summary: The article discusses the realistic dangers of dying in space, comparing common sci-fi tropes with actual scientific understanding. It highlights both accurate portrayals and misconceptions about space fatalities.

title: Blue Origin tests 1st moon lander ahead of lunar launch later this year (photo)
summary: Blue Origin is testing its first moon lander at NASA centers in preparation for a lunar mission later this year. The spacecraft is undergoing various tests to ensure readiness for the upcoming launch.

title: 'Star Fox' is returning this summer with a shiny 'Star Fox 64' remake for Nintendo Switch 2 full of unique features
summary: Nintendo announced a remake of 'Star Fox 64' titled 'Star Fox 64' for the upcoming Nintendo Switch 2, featuring unique enhancements and a return to the Lylat Wars. The game is set to launch this summer.

title: The charred hull of Artemis 2's Orion | Space photo of the day for May 8, 2026
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft has completed its lunar mission and returned to Earth, with its hull charred from re-entry. The spacecraft is now displayed as a space photo of the day.

title: Scientists found 10,000 possible exoplanets hiding in NASA data
summary: Scientists discovered 10,091 potential exoplanets in existing NASA data, which now require further confirmation and analysis.

title: NASA’s SpaceX 34th Commercial Resupply Mission Overview
summary: NASA and SpaceX are targeting a mid-May launch for the 34th Commercial Resupply Mission to the International Space Station, carrying 6,500 pounds of supplies aboard a Dragon spacecraft on a Falcon 9 rocket.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Captures Mars During Gravity Assist Approach
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured a colorized image of Mars during a gravity assist approach on May 3, 2026. The spacecraft will use the gravity assist on May 15 to adjust its trajectory toward asteroid Psyche.

title: I Am Artemis: Anton Kiriwas
summary: Anton Kiriwas, a senior technical integration manager for NASA's Exploration Ground Systems Program, shares his journey and passion for space exploration inspired by an image of the Moon and Mars. The excerpt highlights his career path and involvement in NASA's Artemis program.

title: NASA, Industry Advance High Performance Spaceflight Computing
summary: NASA has been advancing high-performance spaceflight computing for decades, starting with the Apollo Guidance Computers in the 1960s. These radiation-hardened processors have been essential for mission success in space exploration.

title: Glowing Views from the Space Station
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams captured a stunning image of the Milky Way rising above Earth's airglow from the International Space Station aboard a SpaceX Dragon spacecraft. The phenomenon, known as airglow, occurs when atoms and molecules in the upper atmosphere release excess energy as light.

title: NASA Names Brian Hughes to Launch Operations Role
summary: NASA appointed Brian Hughes as senior director of launch operations at Kennedy Space Center. Hughes will oversee NASA's launch infrastructure and report to NASA Headquarters.

title: NASA Fuel Cell Tests Pave Way for Energy Storage on Moon
summary: NASA conducted fuel cell tests to advance energy storage solutions for future lunar missions. The cylindrical fuel cell, equipped with numerous sensors and connections, was carefully positioned for evaluation.

title: Tracy Arm’s Post-Tsunami Landscape
summary: A landslide-triggered tsunami in summer 2025 stripped vegetation from the shore of Tracy Arm, a glacial fjord. The event highlights the environmental impact of natural disasters in remote glacial regions.

title: Meet the Fleet: NASA Armstrong Continues Legacy of Flight Research
summary: NASA's Armstrong Flight Research Center has expanded its fleet with new aircraft, including two F-15 supersonic jets and a Pilatus PC-12 utility plane, continuing its legacy in flight research. The center supports science and aeronautics test missions, building on the work of pioneers like Neil Armstrong.

title: NASA Welcomes Paraguay as 67th Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Paraguay became the 67th signatory to the Artemis Accords, joining the international coalition for civil space exploration. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman welcomed the country's commitment during a ceremony in Asunción.

title: Booster 19 completes Static Fire as Ship 39 prepares for rollout
summary: Booster 19 successfully completed a full-duration, full-thrust static fire test with 33 engines on May 7, 2026. Ship 39 is now preparing for rollout as part of SpaceX's development efforts.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch both cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing its commercial sector towards reusability in deep space missions.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is undergoing conversion as Flight 12 approaches, six months after the completion of Flight 11. The old design of Pad 1 is being upgraded to support future missions.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 flight, potentially marking a period of relative calm before increased activity. The launches are detailed in a preview article from NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its 12th Starship flight test with a revised trajectory. The upcoming mission aims to build on previous test flights with improved performance and safety measures.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully conducted the maiden launch of its new Soyuz-5 rocket on Saturday. The debut flight marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A doctor with experience in mass casualty events and overseas service has been selected as the next NASA astronaut for an upcoming ISS mission. The astronaut discussed their background and preparations for the mission.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return for the heavy-lift vehicle.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the successful Artemis II launch, which carried four astronauts on a lunar flyby mission.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned in the context of space industry news, but no specific details are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) published a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space activities.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical parachute testing process for a space mission, involving a 'bake-out' to ensure the parachute's reliability. This step is essential for the safe return of a spacecraft or payload.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, which includes an in-person event in Paris on June 8-9, 2026, and an online preparatory forum in April-May. The forum aims to gather feedback from smaller enterprises on topics affecting the space sector.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received from an optimal location. The achievement marks a significant milestone for the project.

title: Lunar Outpost Closes $30M Series B, Unveils New Rover
summary: Lunar Outpost raised $30 million in a Series B funding round and unveiled a new rover. The investment reflects investor interest in NASA's plans for lunar exploration.

title: Rocket Lab To Acquire Motiv Space Systems
summary: Rocket Lab will acquire space robotics company Motiv Space Systems, rebranding it as Rocket Lab Robotics upon completion in Q2. The acquisition was announced alongside Rocket Lab's Q1 earnings.

title: HawkEye 360 Raises $416M in IPO
summary: HawkEye 360, a Virginia-based company, raised $416 million in its IPO by pricing 16 million shares at $26 each. The company's S-1 filing in May indicated a price range of $24-$26 per share.

title: SatVu Positions Thermal Imaging as the Missing EO Layer
summary: SatVu has resumed operations, positioning its thermal imaging technology as a crucial missing layer in Earth observation (EO). The company aims to enhance EO capabilities with its advanced thermal imaging satellites.

title: South Korea Pushes to Commercialize Quantum Research
summary: South Korea is aiming to commercialize its quantum research to establish itself in the emerging quantum technology sector. The country seeks to leverage its advancements in quantum research for economic and technological gains.

title: Overview Energy Wins Air Force Contract
summary: Overview Energy has secured an Air Force contract to provide continuous power to military bases using space-based solar power. The company aims to mitigate fuel line vulnerabilities by leveraging solar energy harvested in space.

title: Astranis Raises $450M+, Including $300M Series E
summary: Astranis raised over $450 million in a funding round led by Snowpoint Ventures and Franklin Templeton, valuing the company at $2.8 billion. The $300 million Series E round marks a significant investment in the space industry.

title: Scout Space Closes $18M Series A
summary: Scout Space has secured $18 million in Series A funding. The company plans to expand by building a new satellite sensor factory near Washington, D.C., and nearly doubling its workforce within 18 months.

title: LiveEO Raises €28M+ to Fund its First Constellation
summary: LiveEO, a German company, has raised over €28 million to fund its first satellite constellation. The funds will be used to scale operations, launch the constellation, and develop technology to meet increasing demand for Earth observation data.

title: Commerce Aiming to Open Novel Space Applications This Summer
summary: The Office of Space Commerce (OSC) plans to streamline licensing for novel space missions by this summer. This initiative aims to position OSC as a centralized hub for such applications.

title: Greek Government Taps Planet for New Satellite Data Deal
summary: The Greek government has signed a 2-year, 7-figure contract with Planet for satellite data services. This deal marks a significant expansion of Planet's presence in Europe.

title: Operational AI is Hitting the Limits of Earth Observation Data
summary: AI in Earth Observation is transitioning from experimental use on curated datasets to operational deployment, where models must maintain consistent performance across diverse data sources. This shift highlights the growing challenges in scaling AI applications due to limitations in Earth observation data availability and quality.

title: Globalstar Grows Revenue 17% in Q1 Amid Amazon Acquisition Process
summary: Globalstar reported a 17% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 2026 while in the process of being acquired by Amazon. The company's financial performance coincides with its ongoing acquisition talks.

title: Rocket Lab Q1 Takeaways: Neutron Momentum, SBI Deal and Robotics Acquisition
summary: Rocket Lab reported record quarterly revenue in Q1 2026, highlighting progress on the Neutron rocket, a robotics acquisition, and an SBI deal.

title: Skyroot Secures $60M in Funding, Becoming India’s First Space ‘Unicorn’
summary: Skyroot Aerospace, an Indian launch startup, secured $60 million in funding, becoming India's first space 'unicorn'. The company is a rising star in India's space economy.

title: HawkEye 360 Raises $416M, Pricing at High End for IPO
summary: HawkEye 360 raised $416 million in its initial public offering (IPO), becoming the second space company to go public this year. The signals intelligence company began trading after securing significant funding.

title: Spire Says New German Facility Can Produce Up to 100 Satellites Per Year
summary: Spire has inaugurated a new satellite manufacturing facility in Munich with the capacity to produce up to 100 satellites annually. The facility is designed for end-to-end satellite production.

title: MDA Space Satellite Revenue Grows 40% in Q1, Greenley Says ‘No Change’ Due to Globalstar Deal
summary: MDA Space reported 32% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 2026 and over 40% growth in its satellite manufacturing business, driven by projects like Telesat Lightspeed. CEO Mike Greenley stated that the Globalstar deal did not impact these results.

title: Enabled by AI, NGA Director Focused on Mission, Transformation, and Workforce
summary: The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) is leveraging AI to enhance its mission, transformation, and workforce to address evolving threats and warfighter needs. The agency's new director is prioritizing resilience and the adoption of emerging technologies.

title: Telespazio to Distribute Greenerwave Multi-Orbit Terminals to European Defense Market
summary: Telespazio France will distribute multi-orbit terminals developed by Greenerwave to the European defense market under a new agreement. The deal aims to enhance connectivity solutions for defense applications.
